title: Decoder attention/KV cross-tile reducer measured RTL anchor
hypothesis: The current clustered Llama7B schedule remains tile-attention dominated after analytical command and reducer overhead sweeps. Measuring a standalone cross-tile reducer will calibrate whether owner-cluster and cluster-tree reduction overheads are still small enough to preserve that frontier.

direct_comparison.primary_question: What are the measured Nangate45 timing, area, and power of a ready/valid cross-tile attention reducer for the owner and tree reduction geometries used by the clustered schedule model?
include: perf-sim versus RTL equivalence on reduced values and counters
follow_on_broad_sweep: feed accepted reducer PPA back into the clustered Llama7B schedule estimator
if reducer cost is not negligible, split owner-cluster and tree reducer models

expected_benefit: replaces the reducer overhead proxy with measured circuit-block evidence
calibrates whether the current 2-cluster owner and 8-cluster tree candidates remain plausible
creates a reusable reducer block for later producer-attention-ranker composition
risks: the first block intentionally models fixed-width reduction, not full online softmax normalization
standalone reducer PPA may shift after integration with NoC, SRAM, and command scheduling
large lane counts may require the same macro pin-density handling used by prior wide datapath blocks

required_source_work.objective: Add the source artifacts needed before remote measurement: RTLGen attention_kv_reducer operation, example configs, sweep files, and local RTL equivalence tests.

required_evaluations.objective: Measure one bounded Nangate45 smoke point for a standalone attention/KV reducer block after the RTLGen operation lands.

expected_result.reason: Create the first measured RTL/PPA anchor for the analytical reducer overhead in the clustered schedule model.

required_evaluations.objective: Sweep reducer partial count and lane width for the owner-cluster and cluster-tree overhead candidates.

expected_result.reason: Calibrate the reducer geometry used by owner-cluster and tree-reduction clustered attention schedules.
